title: Reflogs, Reachability Windows, and Recovery · Graph Lens: Gita Lost Tip Discovery
summary: A force update and expired topic branch hide two weeks of work while automatic maintenance approaches; recovery must preserve candidate objects and establish exact provenance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita lost tip discovery increment in reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.
Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Lost Tip Discovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G23 is a theory in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G23 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it. A defensible solution applies Gita Lost Tip Discovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window
git reflog show --date=iso --all
# expected: the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry
# observation: topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature
# changed-case: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it
# transfer-limit: reflogs are local retention aids, not shared audit logs or backup guarantees
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Lost Tip Discovery evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-lost-tip-discovery-1koo6k" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Lost Tip Discovery into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-lost-tip-discovery-1koo6k
# scenario-key: trace-1koo6k probe-k6ook1 boundary-oo6k1k owner-1koo6kx fault-1koo6kz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1k candidate=b00b16k
# hypothesis: the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window
git reflog show --date=iso --all
# expected: the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry
# observation: topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature
# changed-case: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence
# after-ref: main=a11ce1k candidate=c0ffeek
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it
# transfer-limit: reflogs are local retention aids, not shared audit logs or backup guarantees
